name: GRIM.exe

notes: all combat numbers are simulator output on unverified constants
checkpoint levels are provisional (no level timeline in the match feed)
evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias
this build stacks over +100% attack speed and the sim has NO measured attack-speed cap (calibration checklist 7) — sustained-DPS numbers are optimistic until the cap is measured

coachLine: Sim-optimal for sustained DPS: first spike lands around minute 12 (Deathstalker), third item by minute 25. Take Demiurge: +18.7% on your headline output at minute 15.

augments.name: Reboot.exe
augments.note: passive-triggered cooldown refund; trigger cadence unobservable

augments.name: System Restore
augments.note: block-conditional heal and movement; reactive, unmodeled

metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for 10s rotation damage, online around minute 25 — about 732 damage over a 10s rotation in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +37% more 10s rotation damage: try Deathstalker over Lightning Hawk (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 26 — about 959 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +12% more one-combo burst: try Deathstalker over Plasma Blade (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for 10s rotation damage, online around minute 25 — about 769 damage over a 10s rotation in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +30% more 10s rotation damage: try Deathstalker over Lightning Hawk (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 38% of its bite; you give up 30% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 30% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Even kill threat throughout: this lane is decided by mechanics and wave control, not scaling.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 30% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention.
